intro: Today's headline is a first for AI policy: after the White House asked OpenAI to hold GPT-5.6 back for government review, the model went fully public today. Bitcoin, meanwhile, gave back its early-July bounce as the Iran ceasefire collapsed and oil spiked.

items.headline: GitHub ships agentic browser tools and a quarter's worth of Copilot upgrades in VS Code
items.summary: GitHub's roundup of VS Code Copilot releases (v1.123-v1.127) confirms agentic browser tools — letting agents navigate pages, inspect content, and validate web apps directly in the editor — are now generally available, alongside clearer AI cost visibility and Marketplace model discovery.
items.why: Gives coding agents a hands-on way to verify their own web-app changes instead of guessing from static code.

items.headline: Claude Code and Cowork enter public beta for government agencies
items.summary: Anthropic launched a public beta of Claude Code and Claude Cowork through Claude for Government Desktop, running in a FedRAMP High authorized environment with admin spending controls and tamper-evident audit logs for the agency authorization process.
items.why: Opens Anthropic's coding and knowledge-work agents to federal agencies handling sensitive unclassified work, a market OpenAI and Google are also chasing.

items.headline: OpenAI publicly releases GPT-5.6 after Trump administration lifts review restrictions
items.summary: OpenAI began the full public rollout of its GPT-5.6 family — Sol, Terra, and Luna — on Thursday, roughly two weeks after the White House asked it to limit access to about 20 vetted partners while officials reviewed the model under a new AI cybersecurity order. Sol targets frontier coding and cybersecurity work; Terra matches GPT-5.5 at half the cost; Luna is the fastest, cheapest tier.
items.why: The first time the US government has preemptively restricted a frontier model's release before launch — a precedent for how future AI releases get reviewed.

items.headline: Mistral enters robotics with a camera-only navigation model
items.summary: Mistral AI released Robostral Navigate, a robotics navigation model that lets robots move through complex environments using a single camera and plain-language prompts; the French startup trained it entirely in simulation and built it to be hardware-agnostic.
items.why: Signals Mistral's move into 'physical AI' alongside its language models, a space OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and robotics startups are also racing into.

items.headline: VS Code 1.128 adds richer multi-chat agent sessions and GA image support in Chat
items.summary: The latest VS Code release makes it easier to run and manage multiple concurrent agent chat sessions, moves image attachments in Chat to general availability, and adds OS-level keyboard shortcut support.
items.why: Editor-level support for juggling several agent sessions at once reflects how much AI-agent workflows have reshaped the core IDE.

items.headline: GitHub's Innersource security advisories reach general availability
items.summary: GitHub Advanced Security enterprise customers can now use Innersource security advisories, which work like GitHub's public open-source advisories but keep visibility restricted to repositories the enterprise owns.
items.why: Lets large organizations track and disclose vulnerabilities in internal shared code with the same rigor as public CVEs.

items.headline: Bitcoin drops to $62,000 as the Iran ceasefire collapses and oil surges
items.summary: Bitcoin fell about 2.4% to roughly $62,100 after President Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was 'over' and the US and Iran traded fresh airstrikes, sending WTI crude up 5% to above $74 a barrel; ether and XRP fell similarly, and crypto-linked stocks Strategy and Coinbase both declined.
items.why: A reminder that bitcoin still trades as a risk asset in a geopolitical shock, not the uncorrelated hedge some investors want it to be.

items.headline: Spot bitcoin ETFs post their largest daily inflow in over a month
items.summary: US spot bitcoin ETFs took in $265.69 million on Monday, led by BlackRock's IBIT with $209.40 million, marking a third straight day of net inflows after a stretch of outflows in late June.
items.why: A tentative sign institutional demand is turning back on even as the spot price stays choppy.

items.headline: SEC plans to propose 'Regulation Crypto' as soon as this month
items.summary: The SEC's 2026 regulatory agenda lists a new 'Regulation Crypto' rule, still under White House review, that would grant temporary registration exemptions for developers issuing crypto investment contracts and set up a safe harbor for issuers who later step back from managerial control.
items.why: Would be the clearest fundraising rulebook US crypto startups have had since the SEC's stance shifted this year.
